## We use cookies
This website uses cookies which may lead to the personal data processing. Please read our [Privacy Policy](https://sparrowquantum.com/privacy-policy/) to learn more about who we are, how you can contact us, how we process personal data and your user rights. By clicking ‘Accept all’, you accept the use of cookies. [About cookies](javascript:TogglePage\(this,%20'coiPage-3'\);) [Google privacy policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy)

## Cookie policy
**Your consent applies to the following domains:** sparrowquantum.com **The cookie policy was last updated on 15.06.2026**
### What is a cookie?
A cookie is a small data file stored in your computer, tablet or smartphone. A cookie is not a program that can contain harmful malware or virus.
### How our website uses cookies
Some cookies perform essential functions for our website. Cookies also help us get an overview of your visit to our website so we can continuously optimize and tailor the experience to your needs and interests. For example, cookies remember things like whether you have visited our website before; if you are logged in; and the specific language you prefer to see on the website. We also use cookies to target our ads specifically to you on other websites. In general, we use cookies as part of our service to present you with content that is as relevant to you as possible.
You can see the specific services that store cookies and why they do it, under the different categories:
  1. Functional
  2. Statistical
  3. Marketing


### How long are cookies stored?
The length of time a cookie is stored on your devices and browsers varies. The lifetime is calculated according to your last visit to the website. When a cookie expires, it is automatically deleted. All our cookies’ lifetimes are specified in our cookie policy.
### How can you manage cookies?
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission. You can change or withdraw your consent to which cookies you would like to allow by clicking an icon in the bottom left corner of our website.
Additionally, you can clear cookies, prevent cookies, delete cookies and enable cookies at your will and for different circumstances. For details, visit [www.allaboutcookies.org](https://www.allaboutcookies.org/).
### Do you have any questions?
If you have any comments or questions relating to our information and/or processing of personal data, please feel welcome to contact us. The cookie policy itself is updated once a month by [Cookie Information](https://cookieinformation.com/). If you have any questions about our cookie policy, you are welcome to [contact Cookie Information on their website.](https://cookieinformation.com/)
Privacy Controls Accept all

# Sparrow  
Nest
![Black Sparrow Quantum air purifier with vertical blue LED indicator lights and touch control panel.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68bf2168322e23513a20fe03_Sparrow_rack_product.png)
Plug-and-play deterministic single-photon source
Explore
[Technology](https://sparrowquantum.com/technology)
## Deploy single-photons on-demand   
at the touch of a button
Sparrow Nest is a fully integrated, rack-mounted single-photon generator solution powered by Sparrow Core. It offers industry-grade computer control, sensors, and auto-alignment for consistent, high-quality photon output — all at the push of a button.  

### [Laser  
‍ ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbf1c48a2263d2ca67_Asset%201LASER.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-nest#Laser)
### [Optical   
module ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdb566081f3fab8bc11_Asset%202OPTICAL%20MODULE.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-nest#ElectricalModule)
### [Electrical  
module ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbdfc31f3c8edb516b_Asset%203ELECTRICAL%20MODULE.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-nest#OpticalModule)
### [Sparrow   
Core ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdb63cb077f13bacc09_Asset%204SOURCE.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-nest#Sparrow-Core)
### [Cryostat  
‍ ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbcdfe59b23520be15_Asset%205CRYOSTAT.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-nest#Cryostat)
Optimized for quantum computing, quantum communication (QKD/networking), and quantum optics teams that want a plug-and-play system for deterministic single-photon generation.  

## Explore Sparrow Nest
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbf1c48a2263d2ca67_Asset%201LASER.svg)
### Laser
A high-performance, pulsed resonant excitation laser is required to drive Sparrow Core at 80 ± 0.5 MHz; supports lower rates via an external pulse picker (<80 MHz) with dedicated optical in/out.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbdfc31f3c8edb516b_Asset%203ELECTRICAL%20MODULE.svg)
### Electrical module
Industrial-grade computer and control electronics handle automated startup, alignment, calibration, and continuous monitoring for closed-loop thermal/optical stability. Runs as an unattended service and interacts through a served web app or automated with the API.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdbcdfe59b23520be15_Asset%205CRYOSTAT.svg)
### Cryostat
We recommend a compact cryostat to house the Sparrow Core; compressor is placed in an adjacent rack for vibration suppression, supporting stable long-run operation.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdb566081f3fab8bc11_Asset%202OPTICAL%20MODULE.svg)
### Optical module
Precision free-space optics with integrated sensors, camera, and motorized mechanics for stable photon extraction and fiber coupling; single-photon output on PM fiber (PM780, FC/APC) with options (PM980/custom).
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68f73fdb63cb077f13bacc09_Asset%204SOURCE.svg)
### Sparrow Core
InAs-based quantum dot chip providing world-class single-photon metrics (indistinguishability 90–97%, g(2)(0) < 1%, fiber efficiency 30–50%), with fixed per-chip wavelength typically 920–960 nm. Learn more about [Sparrow Core.](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-core)
## Built for seamless deployment in labs and supercomputing centers.
Sparrow Nest is built for advanced labs and HPC sites, and ships as a fully self-contained system in a standard 19-inch rack. It runs on standard power and integrates all subsystems in-rack for quick deployment and reliable day-to-day operation.  

Height: 42U
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68fa20c2f701c5c604fd25c7_Laser%20grid%203.svg)
Depth: 1000 mm
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68fa20c2090c774a369c0a65_laser%20grid%202.svg)
Width: 800 mm
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68fa20c2483fcea9104ad337_Lasre%20gird%201.svg)
![Black Sparrow Quantum server unit with blue illuminated icons and the text 'Enabling Quantum Breakthroughs'.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68fa1fe990631c722adc6f39_Sparrow%20Nest_side%20view_transparent.avif)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
### FAQs about Sparrow Nest
### Each source on the chip delivers exceptional quantum photonic properties: near-unity β-factor for deterministic
Here you’ll find answers to the most common questions about our products, services, and procedures. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to contact us if you have additional questions.
#### How can Sparrow Core be installed?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
For optimal performance, the chip should be mounted in a low-vibration cryostat with both electrical and optical access. The quantum dot is excited by a pulsed laser focused onto the chip through a confocal microscope objective. Single-photon emission is collected via the same objective, while photonic crystal nanostructures integrated into the chip automatically filter out the laser pump light. This ensures the single-photon stream is ready for immediate use. LINK TO DIAGRAM OR APPLICATION NOTE (DO WE HAVE THAT?)  

#### Does the chip require frequent calibration or maintenance? 
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
The source demonstrates robustness to thermal cycling over time. Little to no wavelength variations allow operation over multiple thermal cycles. For more insights, read about one customer’s experience in Vienna (LINK).  

#### Deterministic vs. Probabilistic sources – what to know.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
A deterministic single-photon source provides reliable, on-demand photon emission, ensuring maximum efficiency and scalability for quantum technologies. It enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.
#### What is the efficiency and what can I expect as a count rate? 
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
We typically aim to deliver single-photon sources with efficiencies exceeding 50%. For an 80 MHz pulsed laser, this translates to at least 40 million photons emitted from the chip. The single-photon beam mode can achieve up to 84% overlap with the core of a single-mode fiber, resulting in over 33 million photons coupled into the fiber. The final count rate will depend on additional setup-dependent losses.  

#### What advantages do quantum dots offer for single-photon sources?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Quantum dots are ideal for single-photon sources due to their precise photon control, near-perfect light coupling in photonic nanostructures, and solid-state stability. They minimize decoherence, ensuring high-quality photons for quantum communication, computing, and networking while being compact, scalable, and reliable. READ MORE HERE: "Quantum-dot-based photonic networks"  

### You have questions? We have answers.
Here, you’ll find answers to the most common questions about Sparrow Core. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to [**contact us**](https://sparrowquantum.com/contact) if you have additional questions.
#### What performance should I expect at the fiber output?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Indistinguishability **90–97%** (HOM, 12.5 ns), single-photon purity **g****(2)****(0) < 1%**, fiber efficiency **30–50%** per excitation; typical chip linewidth **< 3 GHz**; lifetime **150–600 ps** (measured at the system’s fiber output).  

#### Can I tune the wavelength or change the repetition rate?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Wavelength is effectively **fixed per quantum dot** within **920–960 nm**. The internal laser runs **80 ± 0.5 MHz** ; lower rates are supported with an **external pulse picker ( <80 MHz)**.  

#### How stable is the system—and how is vibration handled?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Closed-loop thermal/optical control with automated startup, alignment, and calibration keeps brightness drift **< 3% over 5 min** and **< 5% over 12 h**. The cryostat compressor is mounted in an adjacent rack to suppress vibration (see **System Performance**).  

#### What site conditions matter most?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Keep a **stable room temperature** (low drift, non-condensing), ensure a **low-vibration** floor, and provide adequate **power and heat handling** —these are the biggest levers for long-run stability.  

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
#### Designed for quantum researchers and innovators
Engineered as a fully automated turnkey solution, Sparrow Nest delivers single photons via optical fiber — streamlining integration into other setups.
![Close-up of a modern black server unit with blue LED lights in a dimly lit server room with rows of servers on each side.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/69031d355fee90f1a6fe59c7_Sparrow_nest_serverroom.jpg)
![Black device with vertical blue light bars on sides and centered text reading 'SPARROW QUANTUM'.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/68b9a0dbb4b928abf04cc5a0_Rack_img_2.jpg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/69031da104d346bff7828f11_Sparrow_nest_closeup_1.jpg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)

Cookie policy

Your consent applies to the following domains: sparrowquantum.com The cookie policy was last updated on 15.06.2026

What is a cookie?

A cookie is a small data file stored in your computer, tablet or smartphone. A cookie is not a program that can contain harmful malware or virus.

How our website uses cookies

Some cookies perform essential functions for our website. Cookies also help us get an overview of your visit to our website so we can continuously optimize and tailor the experience to your needs and interests. For example, cookies remember things like whether you have visited our website before; if you are logged in; and the specific language you prefer to see on the website. We also use cookies to target our ads specifically to you on other websites. In general, we use cookies as part of our service to present you with content that is as relevant to you as possible.

You can see the specific services that store cookies and why they do it, under the different categories:

  1. Functional
  2. Statistical
  3. Marketing

 

How long are cookies stored?

The length of time a cookie is stored on your devices and browsers varies. The lifetime is calculated according to your last visit to the website. When a cookie expires, it is automatically deleted. All our cookies’ lifetimes are specified in our cookie policy.

How can you manage cookies?

The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission. You can change or withdraw your consent to which cookies you would like to allow by clicking an icon in the bottom left corner of our website.

Additionally, you can clear cookies, prevent cookies, delete cookies and enable cookies at your will and for different circumstances. For details, visit www.allaboutcookies.org.

Do you have any questions?

If you have any comments or questions relating to our information and/or processing of personal data, please feel welcome to contact us. The cookie policy itself is updated once a month by Cookie Information. If you have any questions about our cookie policy, you are welcome to contact Cookie Information on their website.

    Sparrow
    Nest

    Plug-and-play deterministic single-photon source
    Explore
    Technology

    Deploy single-photons on-demand
    at the touch of a button

    Sparrow Nest is a fully integrated, rack-mounted single-photon generator solution powered by Sparrow Core. It offers industry-grade computer control, sensors, and auto-alignment for consistent, high-quality photon output — all at the push of a button.

    Laser
    ‍

    ‍

    Optimized for quantum computing, quantum communication (QKD/networking), and quantum optics teams that want a plug-and-play system for deterministic single-photon generation.

    Explore Sparrow Nest

    Laser

    A high-performance, pulsed resonant excitation laser is required to drive Sparrow Core at 80 ± 0.5 MHz; supports lower rates via an external pulse picker (<80 MHz) with dedicated optical in/out.

    Electrical module

    Industrial-grade computer and control electronics handle automated startup, alignment, calibration, and continuous monitoring for closed-loop thermal/optical stability. Runs as an unattended service and interacts through a served web app or automated with the API.

    Cryostat

    We recommend a compact cryostat to house the Sparrow Core; compressor is placed in an adjacent rack for vibration suppression, supporting stable long-run operation.

    Optical module

    Precision free-space optics with integrated sensors, camera, and motorized mechanics for stable photon extraction and fiber coupling; single-photon output on PM fiber (PM780, FC/APC) with options (PM980/custom).

    Sparrow Core

    InAs-based quantum dot chip providing world-class single-photon metrics (indistinguishability 90–97%, g(2)(0) < 1%, fiber efficiency 30–50%), with fixed per-chip wavelength typically 920–960 nm. Learn more about Sparrow Core.

    Built for seamless deployment in labs and supercomputing centers.

    Sparrow Nest is built for advanced labs and HPC sites, and ships as a fully self-contained system in a standard 19-inch rack. It runs on standard power and integrates all subsystems in-rack for quick deployment and reliable day-to-day operation.
    Height: 42U
    Depth: 1000 mm
    Width: 800 mm

    FAQs about Sparrow Nest

    Each source on the chip delivers exceptional quantum photonic properties: near-unity β-factor for deterministic

    Here you’ll find answers to the most common questions about our products, services, and procedures. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to contact us if you have additional questions.

    How can Sparrow Core be installed?

    For optimal performance, the chip should be mounted in a low-vibration cryostat with both electrical and optical access. The quantum dot is excited by a pulsed laser focused onto the chip through a confocal microscope objective. Single-photon emission is collected via the same objective, while photonic crystal nanostructures integrated into the chip automatically filter out the laser pump light. This ensures the single-photon stream is ready for immediate use. LINK TO DIAGRAM OR APPLICATION NOTE (DO WE HAVE THAT?)

    Does the chip require frequent calibration or maintenance?

    The source demonstrates robustness to thermal cycling over time. Little to no wavelength variations allow operation over multiple thermal cycles. For more insights, read about one customer’s experience in Vienna (LINK).

    Deterministic vs. Probabilistic sources – what to know.

    A deterministic single-photon source provides reliable, on-demand photon emission, ensuring maximum efficiency and scalability for quantum technologies. It enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.

    What is the efficiency and what can I expect as a count rate?

    We typically aim to deliver single-photon sources with efficiencies exceeding 50%. For an 80 MHz pulsed laser, this translates to at least 40 million photons emitted from the chip. The single-photon beam mode can achieve up to 84% overlap with the core of a single-mode fiber, resulting in over 33 million photons coupled into the fiber. The final count rate will depend on additional setup-dependent losses.

    What advantages do quantum dots offer for single-photon sources?

    Quantum dots are ideal for single-photon sources due to their precise photon control, near-perfect light coupling in photonic nanostructures, and solid-state stability. They minimize decoherence, ensuring high-quality photons for quantum communication, computing, and networking while being compact, scalable, and reliable. READ MORE HERE: "Quantum-dot-based photonic networks"

    You have questions? We have answers.

    Here, you’ll find answers to the most common questions about Sparrow Core. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to contact us if you have additional questions.

    What performance should I expect at the fiber output?

    Indistinguishability 90–97% (HOM, 12.5 ns), single-photon purity g(2)(0) < 1%, fiber efficiency 30–50% per excitation; typical chip linewidth <3 GHz; lifetime 150–600 ps (measured at the system’s fiber output).

    Can I tune the wavelength or change the repetition rate?

    Wavelength is effectively fixed per quantum dot within 920–960 nm. The internal laser runs 80 ± 0.5 MHz; lower rates are supported with an external pulse picker (<80 MHz).

    How stable is the system—and how is vibration handled?

    Closed-loop thermal/optical control with automated startup, alignment, and calibration keeps brightness drift <3% over 5 min and <5% over 12 h. The cryostat compressor is mounted in an adjacent rack to suppress vibration (see System Performance).

    What site conditions matter most?

    Keep a stable room temperature (low drift, non-condensing), ensure a low-vibration floor, and provide adequate power and heat handling—these are the biggest levers for long-run stability.

    Designed for quantum researchers and innovators

    Engineered as a fully automated turnkey solution, Sparrow Nest delivers single photons via optical fiber — streamlining integration into other setups.
